subsection.heading: In general
subsection.chapeau: Not later than 1 year after November 15, 2021, the Administrator shall implement measures to expand and improve the international energy data resources of the Energy Information Administration in order to understand—

paragraph.content: the production and use of energy in various countries;
changing patterns of energy use internationally;
the relative costs and environmental impacts of energy production and use internationally; and
plans for or construction of major energy facilities or infrastructure.

subsection.heading: Requirements
subsection.chapeau: In carrying out subsection (a), the Administrator shall—

paragraph.content: work with, and leverage the data resources of, the International Energy Agency;
include detail on energy consumption by fuel, economic sector, and end use within countries for which data are available;

paragraph.chapeau: collect relevant measures of energy use, including—

paragraph.content: provide tools that allow for straightforward country-to-country comparisons of energy production and consumption across economic sectors and end uses.

note.heading: Wage Rate Requirements
note.p: For provisions relating to rates of wages to be paid to laborers and mechanics on projects for construction, alteration, or repair work funded under div. D or an amendment by div. D of Pub. L. 117–58, including authority of Secretary of Labor, see section 18851 of this title.
